name: ANGEL EGGS
intro: Angel eggs are my version of deviled eggs that use Yukon Gold potatoes for the eggs. These are simple to make and make a great brunch appetizer. All you need is a potato, some black salt and some nutritional yeast, and we can make some magic. Ready to impress?

ingredients.item: small Yukon Gold or red potatoes (they should be egg-sized)

method: Fill a large pot with water, add the black salt and bring to a boil. Once boiling, add the Yukon Gold potatoes and cook for 10 minutes, or until fork-tender. Drain the potatoes and cut them in half. Use a small spoon or apple corer to scoop out some of the potatoes from the center to create a small impression for the filling.

Bring a pot of water to a boil with ½ teaspoon of the black salt. Once boiling, add the russet potatoes and cook for 10 minutes, or until fork-tender. Drain the potatoes, remove the skin and place them in a bowl with the remaining ½ teaspoon of black salt, Dijon mustard, relish, nutritional yeast, turmeric, pepper, garlic powder and vegan mayonnaise. Mash until well combined.
Place the filling in a piping bag and gently squeeze the filling into the center of the Yukon potato halves. Garnish with paprika and serve.
